attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: age (40) and sex_at_birth (female) are INFERRED with weak signal — the source note records neither; it gives only Wt 68 kg and a recurrent, stress-linked adult presentation. Both are required demographics fields, so conservative adult values were supplied (age_band 'adult') and MUST be confirmed on clinician review; sex has no source signal at all.
Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-DERM-01-00216) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket via cases:ingest --reseq.

Specialty assigned DERM (mucocutaneous viral infection) as the best in-enum fit and to mirror the sibling oral-lesion case; ID (infectious) or GP are alternative classifications the reviewer may prefer (case_metadata.specialty_tags is revisable at zero cost to the seal).

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source note is an in-person urgent home visit where the clinician performed a dermatological and intraoral examination, palpated submandibular lymphadenopathy, and took full vital signs. Reprojected to recognition from video-observable vesicles + tingling prodrome + recurrent history; the examination findings, intraoral inspection, node palpation and measured vitals are sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits.

Tiering: baseline T2 (benign, telehealth/GP/pharmacist-manageable, oral antiviral needs a script) with minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T1 — deliberately LOW so an AI that correctly gives early antiviral/self-care + infection-control + eye safety-net passes. The source note's MBS urgent-after-hours (585-600) framing is the in-person clinician's billing justification and was NOT used to inflate the tier.

Dose transcribed verbatim from the source note (valaciclovir 2000 mg twice daily for 1 day — the single-day episodic recurrent-herpes-labialis regimen). Adult patient, no paediatric dosing involved; paracetamol and topical-aciclovir directions are standard OTC references, none synthesised beyond the source.

AMT/PBS medication concept codes were NOT assigned to avoid fabricating specific identifiers without a terminology connection; only SNOMED CT / ICD-10-AM diagnosis and differential candidates are registered. Terminology step to add AMT codes for valaciclovir, topical aciclovir and paracetamol.

Differential SNOMED candidates (impetigo, aphthous ulcer, herpes zoster, Stevens-Johnson syndrome, cellulitis) are best-effort candidates pending terminology verification; ICD-10-AM B00.1 is the candidate for herpes labialis (herpesviral vesicular dermatitis).

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
